data.title: Sam Bankman-Fried's latest California prison once housed Al Capone
data.description:
Former FTX CEO Sam “SBF” Bankman-Fried has moved from a transit facility to a California prison that once housed infamous gangster Al Capone. According to the Federal Bureau of Prisons website, officials moved Bankman-Fried from the Federal Transfer Center in Oklahoma City briefly to the Federal Correctional Institution in Victorville before transferring him to a facility in Terminal Island in Los Angeles, California. The federal institution was once home to criminals like former Theranos chief operating officer Ramesh Balwani and Capone, who was convicted of tax evasion in 1931. During his 2023 trial and following his conviction on seven felony counts in 2024, Bankman-Fried was housed at the Metropolitan Detention Center in New York
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/sam-bankman-fried-california-prison-al-capone?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Crypto Takes Off in Panama: City to Accept Bitcoin, Ethereum for Public Services
data.description:
Panama City has embraced a new system for paying public services with Bitcoin, Ethereum, USDC, and USDT, using local bank partnership.
source: https://cryptopotato.com/crypto-takes-off-in-panama-city-to-accept-bitcoin-ethereum-for-public-services/

data.title: Powell says the Fed is ready to provide dollar liquidity to foreign central banks through swap lines
data.description:
Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell warned on Wednesday in Chicago that the U. S. central bank could be forced into a situation where it has to make tough, possibly painful decisions. The warning came as the Federal Reserve tries to deal with economic pressure coming from President Donald Trump’s escalating trade war. Powell added that the […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/powell-tariffs-hitting-harder-than-expected/

data.title: Raydium Debuts Solana Token Launchpad Following Split From Pump.fun
data.description:
LaunchLab by Raydium is now live, offering a more robust Solana token launchpad following a break with former ally Pump. fun.
source: https://decrypt.co/315140/raydium-solana-token-launchpad-pumpfun-split

data.title: U.S. Homeland Security Initiates Probe Into Crypto Bank Anchorage Digital: Report
data.description:
The U. S. Department of Homeland Security is reportedly investigating the cryptocurrency company Anchorage Digital Bank. According to a new Barron’s report, the federal agency’s El Dorado Task Force, which combats money laundering and other financial crimes, has contacted Anchorage Digital Bank employees to inquire about the company’s practices and policies
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/04/16/u-s-homeland-security-initiates-probe-into-crypto-bank-anchorage-digital-report/

data.title: Panama City to accept crypto for taxes, permits and fines via bank conversion
data.description:
Panama City now joins other cities across the globe, such as Detroit, in permitting cryptocurrencies for municipal payments.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/351048/panama-city-to-accept-crypto-for-taxes-permits-and-fines-via-bank-conversion?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: In the past hour, the entire network has exploded more than 60 million US dollars, mainly in long orders
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Coinglass data shows that in the past hour, the entire network has a liquidation of 60.2055 million US dollars, of which long orders have a liquidation of 51.2339 million US dollars and short orders have a liquidation of 8.9715 million US dollars.

data.title: Hana Network has completed a total of US$6 million in financing, and the first phase of the main network, Hanafuda, has been officially launched
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Hana Network, the company behind Hypercasual Finance, announced that it has completed a total of US$6 million in financing. In the recent No More CEX round of financing, core members of the L1/L2 project and some KOLs participated.
In addition, Hana Network's mainnet phase 1 Hanafuda has officially launched. Hanafuda already has 500,000 users and US$40 million TVL, and its mainnet phase 2 and phase 3 are expected to be launched in the next few months.
Earlier news, Hana Network, the company behind Hypercasual Finance, announced that it had completed a new round of financing of US$4 million, with Sushiswap, Alliance and Orange DAO participating.
